organ_toxicities.manifestation: Nephrogenic diabetes insipidus (20-40%), chronic tubulointerstitial nephropathy, ESRD with prolonged use
organ_toxicities.monitoring: Serum creatinine and eGFR every 3-6 months. Urine osmolality if polyuria develops.
organ_toxicities.management: Amiloride for NDI. Consider switching to alternative mood stabilizer if progressive CKD.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Hypothyroidism (20-30%), goiter. Lithium inhibits thyroid hormone synthesis and release.
organ_toxicities.management: Levothyroxine supplementation. Lithium can often be continued.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Tremor (27%), cognitive dulling, ataxia, seizures (in toxicity). Chronic exposure: SILENT syndrome (syndrome of irreversible lithium-effectuated neurotoxicity).
organ_toxicities.monitoring: Serum lithium levels (therapeutic 0.6-1.2 mEq/L). Clinical neurologic assessment.
organ_toxicities.management: Dose adjustment for tremor. Propranolol for tremor if dose can't be reduced.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: T-wave flattening/inversion (benign), sinus node dysfunction, first-degree AV block
organ_toxicities.monitoring: Baseline ECG, then annual ECG for patients >40 or with cardiac risk factors
organ_toxicities.management: Usually benign. Discontinue for significant conduction abnormalities.
May require parathyroidectomy if significant hypercalcemia

overdose_management: Aggressive IV normal saline hydration. Hemodialysis for levels >4.0 mEq/L, >2.5 with severe symptoms, or renal failure. Whole bowel irrigation if sustained-release preparation. Monitor levels q2-4h until declining. No activated charcoal (lithium not adsorbed).
